sourceQuadratureIntervalStatus.label: source Gram interval quadrature bound
sourceQuadratureIntervalStatus.reason: The imported interval envelope for d_u^2(E_u^*E_u), combined with the composite trapezoid factor, gives a nonnegative operator bound for ||S_8-S_{8,h}||.

thetaTailIntervalStatus.label: n>=9 full-theta tail interval propagation
thetaTailIntervalStatus.reason: The omitted full-theta tail contributes a nonnegative operator perturbation bound that is added to the source quadrature error before the Riesz/rank comparison.

totalErrorAssemblyStatus.label: total continuum source error assembly
totalErrorAssemblyStatus.reason: The full source perturbation bound dominates the sum of the interval source quadrature error and the theta-tail operator perturbation.

rieszProjectorStabilityStatus.label: active Riesz projector stability
rieszProjectorStabilityStatus.reason: The total perturbation is below one quarter of the active/complement spectral gap, equivalently alpha<1, so the active two-dimensional Riesz subspace is stable.

activeResponseRankStatus.label: active response rank survives continuum and tail perturbation
activeResponseRankStatus.reason: The finite active response has two positive singular values, and the interval Riesz perturbation leaves a positive lower response margin.

fullThetaSourceNoncollapseStatus.label: full-Phi active source noncollapse
fullThetaSourceNoncollapseStatus.reason: The interval source quadrature bound, theta-tail bound, Riesz projector stability, and positive active response margin prove rank(LE^*|U_delta)=2 for the full-Phi source window.

formalProof: Let S_full be the full-Phi continuum source Gram operator and let S_{8,h} be the certified theta-truncated source Gram.
The interval source quadrature and n>=9 theta-tail certificates give ||S_full-S_{8,h}|| <= totalContinuumErrorBound.
Since totalContinuumErrorBound < spectralGapToComplementS8/4, the active two-dimensional Riesz spectral subspace persists.
The finite active response singular margin, after the Riesz angle loss and complement-response perturbation, remains positive: lowerBoundAfterContinuumAndTail > 0.
Therefore the full-Phi active response map has rank two, equivalently the active source map is noncollapsed on U_delta.
nextProofTarget: Use this interval theorem as the source-rank input to the continuum trace-frame theorem.
